description: Direct DOM manipulation with innerHTML can lead to XSS vulnerabilities when error messages contain user-controlled data.
suggested_fix: Use textContent instead of innerHTML or properly sanitize the error message before insertion.

description: Inconsistent naming between 'ajax' export (which is actually getUrl) and the actual HTTP methods (get, post, etc.).
suggested_fix: Rename 'ajax' export to 'getUrl' to match its actual implementation or document this unusual naming.

description: Duplicated error handling pattern across all HTTP methods (get, post, patch, etc.).
suggested_fix: Create a higher-order function to wrap HTTP methods with consistent error handling.

description: Error re-throwing after handling might lead to double error handling in calling code.
suggested_fix: Either handle the error completely here or let the caller handle it, but not both.

description: Unsafe JSON.parse on localStorage data without validation can lead to prototype pollution or injection attacks.
suggested_fix: Use JSON.parse with try-catch and validate the structure of the parsed data.

description: Hardcoded base URLs in getUrl function makes it difficult to maintain and test different environments.
suggested_fix: Move base URLs to configuration files or environment variables.

description: Direct use of localStorage without validation in getAPIRequestMeta can expose sensitive headers.
suggested_fix: Validate and sanitize user data before using it in headers.

description: Hardcoded error messages might not be internationalized or customizable.
suggested_fix: Consider moving error messages to a configuration or i18n system.

description: handleUnauthorized function doesn't properly handle the error case when isTesting is true.
suggested_fix: Add proper error handling even in test mode, perhaps logging to console.

description: Inconsistent parameter naming between handleAxiosResponseError (displayIfDom) and handleAjaxErrorOnDom (no similar parameter).
suggested_fix: Standardize parameter names across related functions.
